reasoning: The FRED data indicates that the Option-Adjusted Spread for BB-rated high-yield corporate bonds was 1.73 as of December 5, 2025. This is near its 1-year low (the minimum was 1.62 in December 2024), leaving relatively limited room for further downside compared to potential widening. The linear model from the FRED pull suggests a 66% chance of natural mean reversion above this threshold in the near term, although recent momentum over the last week has been downward, bringing the combined estimate closer to 0.50. Given the low base level of 1.73, there is a slightly greater-than-even probability (estimated at 56%) that spreads will widen (increase) in the immediate short-term (December 14). Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase by pulling distant forecasts closer to 0.5, the estimates steadily converge toward 50% for the January 6 and March 7, 2026 resolution dates.
